reasoning:
Based on ACLED data, Togo has seen around 7 'Explosions/Remote violence' events over the 360 days prior to the forecast date (averaging ~0.583 per 30-day window). Under a Poisson distribution, this historical base rate gives a ~44% chance of at least one event occurring in a typical 30-day period. However, recent developments indicate escalating violence. Reports from April 2025 show that JNIM militants have started using more sophisticated tactics, including carrying out the first-ever suicide drone attack on a Togolese military position in Djignandjoaga on April 9. This upward trend suggests the monthly incident rate (lambda) is now likely higher than the historical average, pushing the probability of at least one event occurring in a future 30-day window to around 55-60%.
For the first resolution date (2025-06-01), the 30-day window primarily covers May 2025. Because 23 days of this window have already elapsed with no widely reported major remote violence events, the probability falls below the historical baseline to ~34%, accounting for potential unreported incidents and the remaining week in the window. Probabilities for subsequent dates are based on the higher ~56% forecast reflecting the new drone capabilities of JNIM, but pull closer to 0.5 over longer time horizons as uncertainty increases.
